channel.title: Celeste — From the Bridge
channel.description: Operational thinking for vessel management. Articles on yacht knowledge retention, engineering handovers, and superyacht maintenance intelligence.

item.title: If the System Cannot Compete with WhatsApp, the System Is the Problem
item.description: Yacht crews bypass their maintenance software because it adds friction instead of removing it. Five design failures that cause PMS adoption to collapse on superyachts.

item.title: Seven Connections Every Yacht Maintenance System Should Make (and Almost None Do)
item.description: When evaluating yacht maintenance software, most people check a feature list. But nobody asks the question that actually determines whether the system works: when you open a fault record, what else does the system show you?

item.title: Three Repairs, Three Engineers, One Pattern Nobody Saw
item.description: Superyacht recurring faults stay invisible because crew rotation, siloed systems, and unstructured records prevent fault pattern recognition across time.

item.title: Why Superyacht Maintenance Systems Create the Problems They Claim to Solve
item.description: Siloed yacht maintenance systems store data in parallel but never connect it. Five data silos that cost superyachts money — and why integration is not the same as cross-domain.

item.title: Why Your Warranty Claim Was Denied (and What Documentation Would Have Saved It)
item.description: Most denials are not caused by defective equipment or bad engineering. They are caused by documentation that does not meet the standard the manufacturer requires.

item.title: What a Proper Engineering Handover Looks Like (and Why Most Yachts Don't Have One)
item.description: The engineering handover is the single most important knowledge transfer event on a superyacht. It is also the one with the least standardisation, the shortest time allocation, and the highest cost when it fails.

item.title: When the Chief Engineer Leaves: The Knowledge Crisis on Superyachts
item.description: Every crew rotation on a superyacht is a controlled loss of operational intelligence. How yacht knowledge management fails at crew change, and what proper engineering handover requires.
